title: Is this why we can't get a clear UFO picture?
summary: A brief discussion about why UFO photographs are typically blurry, explaining that craft may manipulate spacetime metrics causing light to bend around them. The speaker suggests that clearly-edged UFO photos are likely fake, while authentic ones show blurred edges due to gravitational distortion.

acronyms.definition: An aerial phenomenon that cannot be immediately identified

key_claims.claim: UFO craft manipulate the spacetime metric, causing light to bend around them and resulting in blurry photographs
If a UFO photograph has a clearly defined edge, it is probably a fake
NASA analysts have confirmed that authentic UFO photos cannot have crisp edges
A small appendage on top of the craft produces a heart-shaped gravitational distortion around it
If you stand underneath a hovering craft and look up, you cannot see it because light bends around it
Gravity bends both light and radio waves around the craft
